title: Multi-source assessment: Iran blockade enters Day 18 with 42 redirections, no kinetic exchange
summary: Info Fusion Center fused OSINT, IMINT and SIGINT reporting to assess that the US naval blockade of Iranian ports has produced 42 vessel redirections, 41 stranded tankers and 69 million barrels stuck offshore through Day 18, with no direct kinetic engagement between US and Iranian naval forces despite Iranian rhetorical pushback.
contents:
# Multi-source assessment: Iran blockade enters Day 18 with 42 redirections, no kinetic exchange

## Assessment Framework
Info Fusion Center synthesised OSINT vessel-tracking, IMINT satellite passes over Iranian and US naval anchorages and SIGINT-derived patrol patterns to produce a Day-18 status report on the US naval blockade of Iranian ports declared April 13. Confidence rating: HIGH for vessel and cargo counts based on CENTCOM official disclosures and AIS-derived tracking; MEDIUM-HIGH for assessment of Iranian intent given Tehran's mixed signalling.

## Key Findings
US forces have redirected 42 vessels attempting to violate the blockade, an increase of 3 over the prior 24 hours. 41 tankers carrying 69 million barrels of Iranian crude (~$6 billion) sit stranded offshore. The USS Gerald Ford carrier strike group stages departure from the theatre while CENTCOM Commander Cooper prepares to brief President Trump on April 30 with new military options including a possible SOF raid on Iran's HEU stockpile.

## Threat Posture
No direct kinetic engagement between US and Iranian naval units occurred during the assessment window. IRGC-Navy fast-attack craft remain in port or near coastal waters, with no offensive sortie pattern detected. Iranian President Pezeshkian on April 30 publicly characterised the blockade as illegal but stopped short of authorising kinetic response.
